title: pinus muricata subsp. borealis
intro: Pinus muricata subsp. borealis, also known as the Monterey pine, is a subspecies of evergreen tree native to the mountainous regions of central and southern California. The medicinal properties of Pinus muricata subsp. borealis include its antibacterial and antifungal properties, which make it useful for treating infections and wounds. The health benefits of this plant include reducing inflammation, improving respiratory health, and acting as an antioxidant, which can help protect against cell damage and promote overall well-being. The herbal preparations of Pinus muricata subsp. borealis include essential oils, extracts, and teas, which can be used to promote relaxation, reduce stress, and support immune function.
